sections.paragraph:
καθηκόντως adv. of the pres. ptc. of καθήκω (q.v.) as is fitting, in accordance w. obligation or duty (Polyb. 5, 9, 6; Plut., Mor. 448e; OGI 90, 28 [II b.c.]; PTebt 793 II, 15 [183 b.c.]; EpArist 181; Philo, Cher. 14) 1 Cl 1:3.—DELG s.v. ἥκω.

adv. adv. = adverb, adverbially

pres. pres. = present

ptc. ptc. = participle

q.v. q.v. = quod vide (which see, see that which has just been mentioned)

Polyb Polyb , III–II b.c.—List 5

Plut Plut , I–II a.d.—List 5

OGI OGI = Orientis Graeci Inscriptiones Selectae—List 3

PTebt PTebt = The Tebtunis Papyri—List 4

EpArist EpArist = Epistle of Aristeas, II b.c.—List 5

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

1 Cl 1 Cl = 1 Clement—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 490
